mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. Key trade-offs include the need for Docker and the limitation of running clusters in a single-node configuration.

steps.title: Install Docker
steps.description:
Docker is required to run Kind. Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.

```bash

sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io

brew install --cask docker

```

> For Ubuntu/Debian systems
> For macOS users
commands: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io

steps.title: Download and Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs the Kind binary to your system, allowing you to create Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux
> For ARM64 on Linux

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind. You can specify the Kubernetes version if needed.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Verify the Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the cluster is running correctly by listing the nodes.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Should show the nodes in the cluster

uninstall.title: Remove the Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Ensure you have backed up any necessary data.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> This will remove the cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This command upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64 && chmod +x ./kind && sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

kind delete cluster

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Replaces the existing Kind binary with the latest version.
> Delete the existing cluster before creating a new one.
> Recreate the cluster with the desired Kubernetes version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the directory containing the Kind binary is added to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure it is running correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster creation process timed out.

**Fix:**
Increase the timeout by using the `--wait` flag with a longer duration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes version not supported'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Kubernetes version is not compatible with the installed Kind version.

**Fix:**
Check the Kind documentation for supported Kubernetes versions and adjust accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 and improved cluster creation performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with your node listed under 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker.io

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running before proceeding with Kind installation.
